464	def _resolve_org_id(cur, organization_slug: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
465	    if not organization_slug:
466	        return None
467	    cur.execute(
468	        'SELECT id FROM "Organization" WHERE slug = %s',
469	        (organization_slug,),
470	    )
471	    row = cur.fetchone()
472	    if not row:
473	        raise click.ClickException(
474	            f"Organization slug not found: {organization_slug}"
475	        )
476	    return row["id"] if isinstance(row, dict) else row[0]
477	
478	
479	def _conclusions_with_profiles(cur, *, organization_id: Optional[str], limit: int):
480	    """Conclusions that have at least one MethodologyProfile attached, either
481	    directly or via the upload bridge."""
482	    cur.execute(
483	        '''SELECT DISTINCT c.id AS id,
484	                  c."organizationId" AS org_id,
485	                  c.text             AS text,
486	                  c."topicHint"      AS topic_hint
487	             FROM "Conclusion" c
488	             JOIN "MethodologyProfile" mp
489	                  ON mp."conclusionId" = c.id
490	                  OR mp."uploadId" IN (
491	                       SELECT cs."uploadId" FROM "ConclusionSource" cs
492	                        WHERE cs."conclusionId" = c.id
493	                  )
494	            WHERE (%s IS NULL OR c."organizationId" = %s)
495	            ORDER BY c.id
496	            LIMIT %s''',
497	        (organization_id, organization_id, int(limit)),
498	    )
499	    return list(cur.fetchall())
500	
501	
502	def _profiles_for(cur, *, organization_id: str, conclusion_id: str):
503	    from noosphere.evaluation.mqs import MethodologyProfileSummary
504	
505	    cur.execute(
506	        '''SELECT mp."patternType", mp.title, mp.summary,
507	                  mp."reasoningMoves", mp."transferTargets",
508	                  mp.assumptions, mp."failureModes", mp.confidence
509	             FROM "MethodologyProfile" mp
510	             LEFT JOIN "ConclusionSource" cs ON cs."uploadId" = mp."uploadId"
511	            WHERE mp."organizationId" = %s
512	              AND (mp."conclusionId" = %s OR cs."conclusionId" = %s)
513	            ORDER BY mp.confidence DESC
514	            LIMIT 8''',
515	        (organization_id, conclusion_id, conclusion_id),
516	    )
517	
518	    def _aslist(raw):
519	        if raw is None:
520	            return []
521	        if isinstance(raw, list):
522	            return [str(x) for x in raw if x is not None]
523	        if isinstance(raw, str):
524	            try:
525	                v = json.loads(raw)
526	                return [str(x) for x in v if x is not None] if isinstance(v, list) else []
527	            except Exception:
528	                return []
529	        return []
530	
531	    out = []
532	    for row in cur.fetchall():
533	        out.append(
534	            MethodologyProfileSummary(
535	                pattern_type=(row.get("patternType") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[0]) or "",
536	                title=(row.get("title") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[1]) or "",
537	                summary=(row.get("summary") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[2]) or "",
538	                reasoning_moves=_aslist(
539	                    row.get("reasoningMoves") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[3]
540	                ),
541	                transfer_targets=_aslist(
542	                    row.get("transferTargets") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[4]
543	                ),
544	                assumptions=_aslist(
545	                    row.get("assumptions") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[5]
546	                ),
547	                failure_modes=_aslist(
548	                    row.get("failureModes") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[6]
549	                ),
550	                confidence=float(
551	                    (row.get("confidence") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[7]) or 0.5
552	                ),
553	            )
554	        )
555	    return out
556	
557	
558	@cli.command("track-record")
559	@click.option(
560	    "--organization-slug",
561	    type=str,
562	    default=None,
563	    help="Restrict to a single organization (default: all orgs).",
564	)
565	@click.option(
566	    "--rebuild/--no-rebuild",
567	    default=False,
568	    help="Run the linker + aggregator pipeline before printing. Idempotent.",
569	)
570	@click.option(
571	    "--judge",
572	    "judge_name",
573	    type=click.Choice(["stub"]),
574	    default="stub",
575	    help="Linker judge to use. Only the deterministic stub is exposed via "
576	    "the CLI; production callers may construct their own MethodLinkerJudge.",
577	)
578	@click.option(
579	    "--limit",
580	    type=int,
581	    default=500,
582	    help="Maximum conclusions to (re)link in this run.",
583	)
584	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
585	def track_record(
586	    organization_slug: Optional[str],
587	    rebuild: bool,
588	    judge_name: str,
589	    limit: int,
590	    as_json: bool,
591	) -> None:
592	    """Show per-method track record. With --rebuild, first re-link every
593	    conclusion to its methods and re-aggregate every (method, domain) cell.
594	    Both passes are idempotent: re-running on a quiet DB is a no-op modulo
595	    `updatedAt`."""
596	    from noosphere.evaluation.method_outcome_linker import (
597	        StubMethodLinkerJudge,
598	        infer_links,
599	        registry_view,
600	        upsert_links,
601	    )
602	    from noosphere.evaluation.method_track_record import (
603	        aggregate,
604	        fetch_method_keys,
605	        fetch_resolved_rows,
606	        upsert_track_record,
607	    )
608	
609	    conn, real_dict_cursor = _open_codex()
610	    try:
611	        cur = conn.cursor(cursor_factory=real_dict_cursor)
612	        organization_id = _resolve_org_id(cur, organization_slug)
613	
614	        if rebuild:
615	            registry = registry_view()
616	            judge = StubMethodLinkerJudge()
617	            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
618	
619	            conclusions = _conclusions_with_profiles(
620	                cur, organization_id=organization_id, limit=limit
621	            )
622	            linked = 0
623	            for c in conclusions:
624	                cid = c["id"] if isinstance(c, dict) else c[0]
625	                org = c["org_id"] if isinstance(c, dict) else c[1]
626	                text = (c.get("text") if isinstance(c, dict) else c[2]) or ""
627	                topic = (c.get("topic_hint") if isinstance(c, dict) else c[3]) or ""
628	                profiles = _profiles_for(cur, organization_id=org, conclusion_id=cid)
629	                if not profiles:
630	                    continue
631	                links = infer_links(
632	                    conclusion_id=cid,
633	                    conclusion_text=text,
634	                    topic_hint=topic,
635	                    profiles=profiles,
636	                    registry_methods=registry,
637	                    judge=judge,
638	                )
639	                if not links:
640	                    continue
641	                linked += upsert_links(
642	                    cur,
643	                    organization_id=org,
644	                    conclusion_id=cid,
645	                    links=links,
646	                    now=now,
647	                )
648	
649	            # Aggregate per (org, method, version). Walk every distinct
650	            # method seen in ConclusionMethod so we don't miss methods
651	            # that no longer match a registered name.
652	            cur.execute(
653	                'SELECT id FROM "Organization"'
654	                + (' WHERE id = %s' if organization_id else ''),
655	                (organization_id,) if organization_id else (),
656	            )
657	            org_ids = [
658	                (row.get("id") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[0])
659	                for row in cur.fetchall()
660	            ]
661	            aggregated = 0
662	            for org_id in org_ids:
663	                for (m_name, m_version) in fetch_method_keys(
664	                    cur, organization_id=org_id
665	                ):
666	                    rows = fetch_resolved_rows(
667	                        cur,
668	                        organization_id=org_id,
669	                        method_name=m_name,
670	                        method_version=m_version,
671	                    )
672	                    records = aggregate(
673	                        organization_id=org_id,
674	                        method_name=m_name,
675	                        method_version=m_version,
676	                        rows=rows,
677	                        now=now,
678	                    )
679	                    # Even when rows is empty, emit a sample_size=0 row
680	                    # for every (method, domain="") so downstream UI sees
681	                    # the method exists but has no record yet.
682	                    if not records:
683	                        records = aggregate(
684	                            organization_id=org_id,
685	                            method_name=m_name,
686	                            method_version=m_version,
687	                            rows=[],
688	                            now=now,
689	                        )
690	                        if not records:
691	                            from noosphere.evaluation.method_track_record import (
692	                                TrackRecord,
693	                                TRACK_RECORD_SCHEMA,
694	                            )
695	
696	                            records = [
697	                                TrackRecord(
698	                                    organization_id=org_id,
699	                                    method_name=m_name,
700	                                    method_version=m_version,
701	                                    domain="",
702	                                    sample_size=0,
703	                                    weighted_brier=None,
704	                                    calibration_slope=None,
705	                                    calibration_slope_ci_low=None,
706	                                    calibration_slope_ci_high=None,
707	                                    severity_pass_rate=None,
708	                                    evidence={
709	                                        "schema": TRACK_RECORD_SCHEMA,
710	                                        "prediction_ids": [],
711	                                        "conclusion_ids": [],
712	                                    },
713	                                    computed_at=now,
714	                                )
715	                            ]
716	                    for record in records:
717	                        upsert_track_record(cur, record)
718	                        aggregated += 1
719	            conn.commit()
720	            click.echo(
721	                json.dumps(
722	                    {"linked": linked, "aggregated": aggregated, "judge": judge_name},
723	                    indent=2,
724	                )
725	            )
726	
727	        # Print whatever is in MethodTrackRecord (post-rebuild or cached).
728	        cur.execute(
729	            '''SELECT "organizationId", "methodName", "methodVersion", domain,
730	                      "sampleSize", "weightedBrier",
731	                      "calibrationSlope", "calibrationSlopeCiLow",
732	                      "calibrationSlopeCiHigh", "severityPassRate",
733	                      "computedAt"
734	                 FROM "MethodTrackRecord"
735	                WHERE (%s IS NULL OR "organizationId" = %s)
736	                ORDER BY "methodName", "methodVersion", domain''',
737	            (organization_id, organization_id),
738	        )
739	        rows = list(cur.fetchall())
740	
741	        if as_json:
742	            click.echo(
743	                json.dumps(
744	                    [
745	                        {
746	                            "organization_id": r["organizationId"]
747	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[0],
748	                            "method_name": r["methodName"]
749	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[1],
750	                            "method_version": r["methodVersion"]
751	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[2],
752	                            "domain": r["domain"] if isinstance(r, dict) else r[3],
753	                            "sample_size": r["sampleSize"]
754	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[4],
755	                            "weighted_brier": r["weightedBrier"]
756	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[5],
757	                            "calibration_slope": r["calibrationSlope"]
758	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[6],
759	                            "calibration_slope_ci_low": r["calibrationSlopeCiLow"]
760	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[7],
761	                            "calibration_slope_ci_high": r["calibrationSlopeCiHigh"]
762	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[8],
763	                            "severity_pass_rate": r["severityPassRate"]
764	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[9],
765	                            "computed_at": str(
766	                                r["computedAt"] if isinstance(r, dict) else r[10]
767	                            ),
768	                        }
769	                        for r in rows
770	                    ],
771	                    indent=2,
772	                    default=str,
773	                )
774	            )
775	            return
776	
777	        if not rows:
778	            console.print(
779	                "[yellow]No track records yet. Run with --rebuild.[/yellow]"
780	            )
781	            return
782	
783	        table = Table(title="Method Track Records", show_header=True)
784	        table.add_column("Method", style="cyan")
785	        table.add_column("v")
786	        table.add_column("Domain")
787	        table.add_column("n")
788	        table.add_column("Brier")
789	        table.add_column("Slope")
790	        table.add_column("CI")
791	        table.add_column("Sev pass")
792	        for r in rows:
793	            def _g(key, idx):
794	                return r[key] if isinstance(r, dict) else r[idx]
795	
796	            slope = _g("calibrationSlope", 6)
797	            lo = _g("calibrationSlopeCiLow", 7)
798	            hi = _g("calibrationSlopeCiHigh", 8)
799	            ci = (
800	                f"[{lo:.2f}, {hi:.2f}]"
801	                if (lo is not None and hi is not None)
802	                else "—"
803	            )
804	            brier = _g("weightedBrier", 5)
805	            sev = _g("severityPassRate", 9)
806	            table.add_row(
807	                str(_g("methodName", 1)),
808	                str(_g("methodVersion", 2)),
809	                str(_g("domain", 3) or "—"),
810	                str(_g("sampleSize", 4)),
811	                f"{brier:.3f}" if brier is not None else "—",
812	                f"{slope:.2f}" if slope is not None else "—",
813	                ci,
814	                f"{sev:.0%}" if sev is not None else "—",
815	            )

file.content:
brief = orch.research_advisor.generate_research_brief(
                    episode=ep,
                    claims=claims,
                    new_principles=all_principles[:15],
                    contradictions=contradictions,
                    all_principles=all_principles,
                )

            if json_out:
                import json as json_mod
                console.print(json_mod.dumps(orch.research_advisor.brief_to_dict(brief), indent=2))
            else:
                console.print(f"\n[bold green]✓ Research brief generated: {len(brief.topics)} topics[/bold green]")
                for i, t in enumerate(brief.topics, 1):
                    prio_color = {"high": "red", "medium": "yellow", "exploratory": "green"}.get(t.priority, "white")
                    console.print(f"\n  [{prio_color}]{i}. {t.title}[/{prio_color}]")
                    console.print(f"     [dim]{t.philosophical_question}[/dim]")
                    console.print(f"     [dim]Readings: {len(t.readings)} | Empirical anchors: {len(t.empirical_anchors)}[/dim]")
                console.print(f"\n  [dim]Full brief saved to: {orch.data_dir / 'synthesis' / 'research_briefs'}[/dim]\n")
            return

        # Default: show help
        console.print("\n[bold]Research Advisor[/bold]")
        console.print("  Use --list to see available briefs")
        console.print("  Use --episode N to view a specific brief")
        console.print("  Use --generate to create a fresh brief from current graph state")
        console.print()

    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[bold red]Research advisor error: {e}[/bold red]")
        raise SystemExit(1)


# ── Plugin discovery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
from noosphere.cli_commands import register_commands

cli.add_command(forecasts_cli)
register_commands(cli)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    cli()

content.content:
1	"""Plugin loader for CLI command modules.
2	
3	Every Python module in this package that exposes a top-level Click group
4	named ``cli`` is automatically discovered and attached to the root CLI
5	group.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import importlib
11	import pkgutil
12	import sys
13	import types
14	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    import click
18	
19	
20	def register_commands(root_cli: click.Group) -> None:
21	    """Import every sibling module and attach its ``cli`` group to *root_cli*."""
22	    _install_rich_fallback_if_needed()
23	    package = importlib.import_module(__name__)
24	    for _importer, modname, _ispkg in pkgutil.iter_modules(package.__path__):
25	        mod = importlib.import_module(f"{__name__}.{modname}")
26	        group = getattr(mod, "cli", None)
27	        if group is not None:
28	            root_cli.add_command(group)
29	
30	
31	def _install_rich_fallback_if_needed() -> None:
32	    try:
33	        import rich  # noqa: F401
34	        return
35	    except ImportError:
36	        pass
37	
38	    class Console:
39	        def print(self, *args, **_kwargs) -> None:
40	            print(" ".join(str(arg) for arg in args))
41	
42	    class Table:
43	        def __init__(self, title: str = "", show_header: bool = True, **_kwargs) -> None:
44	            self.title = title
45	            self.rows: list[tuple[str, ...]] = []
46	
47	        def add_column(self, *_args, **_kwargs) -> None:
48	            return None
49	
50	        def add_row(self, *args, **_kwargs) -> None:
51	            self.rows.append(tuple(str(arg) for arg in args))
52	
53	        def __str__(self) -> str:
54	            lines = [self.title] if self.title else []
55	            lines.extend(" | ".join(row) for row in self.rows)
56	            return "\n".join(lines)
57	
58	    rich_mod = types.ModuleType("rich")
59	    console_mod = types.ModuleType("rich.console")
60	    table_mod = types.ModuleType("rich.table")
61	    console_mod.Console = Console
62	    table_mod.Table = Table
63	    rich_mod.console = console_mod
64	    rich_mod.table = table_mod
65	    sys.modules.setdefault("rich", rich_mod)
66	    sys.modules.setdefault("rich.console", console_mod)
67	    sys.modules.setdefault("rich.table", table_mod)
68

836	    # Classification — pydantic v2 defaults to `extra='ignore'`, which means
837	    # constructor calls like `Claim(claim_type=ClaimType.METHODOLOGICAL, ...)`
838	    # scattered across voices.py, adversarial.py, literature.py were
839	    # silently dropping the field. ClaimTypeVerifier even sets

844	    claim_type_verified: Optional[ClaimType] = None
845	    claim_type_disagreement: bool = False
846	    # Per-claim "because/see also" pointers. Concretely these are the
847	    # contradiction-pair IDs (Dialectic session ingest), citation anchors
848	    # (Papers / PDFs), or transcript segment IDs that originally grounded
849	    # the extraction. Omitted from the schema caused silent data loss:
850	    # `ingest_artifacts.ingest_dialectic_session_jsonl` was already
851	    # passing `evidence_pointers=…` to this constructor, but pydantic v2
852	    # drops unknown fields by default, so the round-trip lost them.
853	    evidence_pointers: list[str] = []
854	    confidence_hedges: list[str] = []
855	    # Transcript alignment — used by ingest_papers and ingest_dialectic

857	    chunk_id: str = ""

end_time: Optional[float] = None
    episode_id: str = ""


class Claim(BaseModel):
    """
    An atomic proposition extracted from a transcript segment.

    This is the fundamental unit of analysis. A claim is a single
    assertoric sentence that can be true or false, attributed to a
    speaker, and positioned in embedding space.
    """

    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    text: str  # The proposition itself
    speaker: Speaker  # Who said it
    episode_id: str  # Which episode
    episode_date: date  # When
    segment_context: str = ""  # Surrounding paragraph for disambiguation
    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []  # Relevant domains
    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None  # SBERT embedding vector
    confidence: float = 1.0  # Extraction confidence (0-1)
    timestamp_seconds: Optional[float] = None  # Position in episode
    source_id: str = ""  # Source artifact or chunk ID
    source_type: Optional[InputSourceType] = None
    source_span_start: Optional[int] = None
    source_span_end: Optional[int] = None
    voice_id: str = ""  # Voice profile ID
    founder_id: str = ""
    author_key: str = ""
    effective_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    effective_at_inferred: bool = True
    superseded_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    # Round 3 additions
    freshness: Freshness = Freshness.FRESH
    last_validated_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    # Classification — pydantic v2 defaults to `extra='ignore'`, which means
    # constructor calls like `Claim(claim_type=ClaimType.METHODOLOGICAL, ...)`
    # scattered across voices.py, adversarial.py, literature.py were
    # silently dropping the field. ClaimTypeVerifier even sets
    # `claim.claim_type_verified` / `claim.claim_type_disagreement` at
    # runtime, which would AttributeError on read without these.
    claim_type: ClaimType = ClaimType.FACTUAL
    claim_origin: ClaimOrigin = ClaimOrigin.FOUNDER
    claim_type_verified: Optional[ClaimType] = None
    claim_type_disagreement: bool = False
    # Per-claim "because/see also" pointers. Concretely these are the
    # contradiction-pair IDs (Dialectic session ingest), citation anchors
    # (Papers / PDFs), or transcript segment IDs that originally grounded
    # the extraction. Omitted from the schema caused silent data loss:
    # `ingest_artifacts.ingest_dialectic_session_jsonl` was already
    # passing `evidence_pointers=…` to this constructor, but pydantic v2
    # drops unknown fields by default, so the round-trip lost them.
    evidence_pointers: list[str] = []
    confidence_hedges: list[str] = []
    # Transcript alignment — used by ingest_papers and ingest_dialectic
    # to thread claims back to their source chunks for traceability.
    chunk_id: str = ""


class Principle(BaseModel):
    """
    A distilled, stable belief held by the firm.

    Principles are derived from clusters of claims across multiple episodes.
    They represent the firm's enduring intellectual commitments — the things
    the founders believe deeply enough to act on repeatedly.
    """

    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    text: str  # Canonical statement
    description: str = ""  # Elaborated explanation
    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []
    conviction: ConvictionLevel = ConvictionLevel.MODERATE
    conviction_score: float = 0.5  # Continuous 0-1
    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None

    # Evidence trail
    supporting_claims: list[str] = []  # Claim IDs
    first_appeared: Optional[date] = None
    last_reinforced: Optional[date] = None
    mention_count: int = 0

    # Coherence metrics (from the 6-layer engine)
    coherence_score: Optional[float] = None  # Composite Coh(Γ)
    consistency_score: Optional[float] = None  # S₁: formal consistency
    argumentation_score: Optional[float] = None  # S₂: grounded extension ratio
    probabilistic_score: Optional[float] = None  # S₃: Roche's measure
    geometric_score: Optional[float] = None  # S₄: embedding coherence
    compression_score: Optional[float] = None  # S₅: information-theoretic

    # Metadata
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
    tags: list[str] = []


class Relationship(BaseModel):
    """A directed edge in the knowledge graph."""

    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    source_id: str  # Principle or Claim ID
    target_id: str
    relation: RelationType
    strength: float = 1.0  # 0-1, how strong the relationship
    evidence: str = ""  # Why this relationship exists
    detected_by: str = "extraction"  # extraction | coherence | geometric | manual
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)


class Episode(BaseModel):
    """Metadata for a single podcast episode."""

    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    number: int
    date: date
    title: str = ""
    duration_seconds: Optional[float] = None
    transcript_path: str = ""
    speakers: list[Speaker] = []

    def insert_review_report(self, report: ReviewReport) -> None:
        row = StoredReviewReport(
            report_id=report.report_id,
            conclusion_id=report.conclusion_id,
            payload_json=report.model_dump_json(),
        )
        with self.session() as s:

            s.commit()

    def list_review_reports(self, conclusion_id: str) -> list[ReviewReport]:
        with self.session() as s:
            rows = s.exec(
                select(StoredReviewReport).where(
                    StoredReviewReport.conclusion_id == conclusion_id
                )
            ).all()
            return [ReviewReport.model_validate_json(r.payload_json) for r in rows]

    def insert_rebuttal(self, rebuttal: Rebuttal, *, report_id: str) -> None:
        row = StoredRebuttal(
            id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            report_id=report_id,
            finding_id=rebuttal.finding_id,
            payload_json=rebuttal.model_dump_json(),
        )
        with self.session() as s:
            s.add(row)
            s.commit()

    def list_rebuttals(self, report_id: str) -> list[Rebuttal]:
        with self.session() as s:
            rows = s.exec(
                select(StoredRebuttal).where(StoredRebuttal.report_id == report_id)
            ).all()
            return [Rebuttal.model_validate_json(r.payload_json) for r in rows]

    # ── Round 3: Decay / Revalidation ─────────────────────────────────────

    def insert_decay_policy(self, policy: DecayPolicy) -> str:
        pid = str(uuid.uuid4())
        row = StoredDecayPolicy(id=pid, payload_json=policy.model_dump_json())
        with self.session() as s:
            s.add(row)
            s.commit()
        return pid

    def bind_policy(self, object_id: str, policy_id: str) -> None:
        row = StoredObjectPolicyBinding(
            id=f"{object_id}:{policy_id}",
            object_id=object_id,
            policy_id=policy_id,
        )
        with self.session() as s:
            s.merge(row)
            s.commit()

    def unbind_policy(self, object_id: str, policy_id: str) -> None:
        bid = f"{object_id}:{policy_id}"
        with self.session() as s:
            r = s.get(StoredObjectPolicyBinding, bid)
            if r is not None:
                s.delete(r)
                s.commit()

    def insert_revalidation(self, result: RevalidationResult) -> None:
        row = StoredRevalidation(
            id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            object_id=result.object_id,
            payload_json=result.model_dump_json(),
        )
        with self.session() as s:
            s.add(row)

3184	            s.add(r)

3187	    def iter_cascade_edges(
3188	        self,
3189	        src: Optional[str] = None,
3190	        dst: Optional[str] = None,
3191	        relation: Optional[str] = None,
3192	        include_retracted: bool = False,
3193	    ) -> Iterator[CascadeEdge]:
3194	        with self.session() as s:
3195	            stmt = select(StoredCascadeEdge)
3196	            if src is not None:
3197	                stmt = stmt.where(StoredCascadeEdge.src == src)
3198	            if dst is not None:

3200	            if relation is not None:
3201	                stmt = stmt.where(StoredCascadeEdge.relation == relation)
3202	            if not include_retracted:
3203	                stmt = stmt.where(StoredCascadeEdge.retracted_at == None)  # noqa: E711
3204	            rows = s.exec(stmt).all()
3205	        for r in rows:
3206	            yield CascadeEdge.model_validate_json(r.payload_json)

class CascadeNodeKind(str, Enum):
    """Type of node in the cascade graph."""

    ARTIFACT = "artifact"
    CHUNK = "chunk"
    CLAIM = "claim"
    PRINCIPLE = "principle"
    CLUSTER = "cluster"
    CONCLUSION = "conclusion"
    EXTERNAL_SOURCE = "external_source"


class CascadeNode(BaseModel):
    """A node in the cascade graph."""

    node_id: str
    kind: CascadeNodeKind
    ref: str
    attrs: dict

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="forbid", frozen=True)


class CascadeEdgeRelation(str, Enum):
    """Relation type for cascade edges."""

    EXTRACTED_FROM = "extracted_from"
    COHERES_WITH = "coheres_with"
    CONTRADICTS = "contradicts"
    AGGREGATES = "aggregates"
    REFORMULATES = "reformulates"
    GENERALIZES = "generalizes"
    SPECIALIZES = "specializes"
    PREDICTS = "predicts"
    SUPPORTS = "supports"
    REFUTES = "refutes"
    DEPENDS_ON = "depends_on"


class CascadeEdge(BaseModel):
    """
    A directed edge in the cascade graph.

    NOTE: This model is NOT frozen because retracted_at must be mutable
    for retraction operations.
    """

    edge_id: str
    src: str
    dst: str
    relation: CascadeEdgeRelation
    method_invocation_id: str
    confidence: float
    unresolved: bool
    established_at: datetime
    retracted_at: Optional[datetime] = None

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="forbid")


# ── Round 3: Evaluation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────


class OutcomeKind(str, Enum):
    """Type of outcome for evaluation."""

    BINARY = "binary"
    INTERVAL = "interval"
    PREFERENCE = "preference"

776	class Store:
777	    """Typed CRUD facade over SQLAlchemy.
778	
779	    Supports both SQLite (local dev; ``sqlite:///./noosphere.db``) and
780	    Postgres (``postgresql://…``, e.g. Supabase / Neon / local Docker).
781	    The SQLite-specific ALTER TABLE migrations below short-circuit on
782	    non-sqlite URLs — Postgres gets its schema from
783	    ``SQLModel.metadata.create_all`` and the Alembic revisions under
784	    ``noosphere/alembic/``. Prefer Alembic in production.
785	    """
786	
787	    def __init__(self, engine: Engine) -> None:
788	        self.engine = engine
789	
790	    @classmethod
791	    def from_database_url(cls, url: str) -> Store:
792	        connect_args: dict[str, Any] = {}
793	        if url.startswith("sqlite"):
794	            connect_args["check_same_thread"] = False
795	        engine_url = _psycopg2_compatible_url(url.strip())
796	        eng = create_engine(
797	            engine_url,
798	            connect_args=connect_args,
799	            **_engine_kwargs_for_url(engine_url),
800	        )
801	        SQLModel.metadata.create_all(eng)
802	        _sqlite_migrate_temporal_columns(eng)
803	        _sqlite_migrate_literature_columns(eng)
804	        _sqlite_migrate_currents_columns(eng)
805	        _sqlite_migrate_forecast_columns(eng)
806	        _sqlite_migrate_publication_columns(eng)
807	        _backfill_artifact_effective_times(eng)
808	        _seed_embedding_model_versions(eng)
809	        return cls(eng)
810	
811	    @contextmanager
812	    def session(self) -> Generator[Session, None, None]:
813	        with Session(self.engine) as s:
814	            yield s
815	
816	    # --- Artifact ---
817	    def put_artifact(self, a: Artifact) -> None:
818	        eff = a.effective_at
819	        eff_inferred = a.effective_at_inferred
820	        if eff is None:
821	            if a.source_date:
822	                d = a.source_date
823	                eff = datetime(d.year, d.month, d.day, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
824	                eff_inferred = False
825	            else:
826	                eff = _dt(a.created_at)
827	                eff_inferred = True
828	        row = StoredArtifact(
829	            id=a.id,
830	            uri=a.uri,
831	            mime_type=a.mime_type,
832	            byte_length=a.byte_length,
833	            content_sha256=a.content_sha256,
834	            title=a.title,
835	            author=a.author,
836	            source_date_iso=a.source_date.isoformat() if a.source_date else "",
837	            created_at=_dt(a.created_at),
838	            effective_at=eff,
839	            superseded_at=_dt(a.superseded_at) if a.superseded_at else None,
840	            effective_at_inferred=eff_inferred,
841	            license_status=a.license_status or "unknown",
842	            literature_connector=a.literature_connector or "",
843	        )
844	        with self.session() as s:
845	            existing = s.get(StoredArtifact, a.id)
846	            if existing:
847	                for k, v in row.model_dump(exclude={"id"}).items():
848	                    setattr(existing, k, v)
849	                s.add(existing)
850	            else:
851	                s.add(row)
852	            s.commit()
853	
854	    def get_artifact(self, artifact_id: str) -> Optional[Artifact]:
855	        with self.session() as s:
856	            r = s.get(StoredArtifact, artifact_id)
857	            if r is None:
858	                return None
859	            sd: Optional[date] = None
860	            if r.source_date_iso:
861	                try:
862	                    sd = date.fromisoformat(r.source_date_iso[:10])
863	                except ValueError:
864	                    sd = None
865	            eff = getattr(r, "effective_at", None)
866	            if eff is not None and eff.tzinfo is None:
867	                eff = eff.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
868	            sup = getattr(r, "superseded_at", None)
869	            if sup is not None and sup.tzinfo is None:
870	                sup = sup.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
871	            return Artifact(
872	                id=r.id,
873	                uri=r.uri,
874	                mime_type=r.mime_type,
875	                byte_length=r.byte_length,
876	                content_sha256=r.content_sha256,
877	                title=r.title,
878	                author=r.author,
879	                source_date=sd,
880	                created_at=r.created_at,
881	                effective_at=eff,
882	                superseded_at=sup,
883	                effective_at_inferred=bool(getattr(r, "effective_at_inferred", True)),
884	                license_status=str(getattr(r, "license_status", None) or "unknown"),
885	                literature_connector=str(
886	                    getattr(r, "literature_connector", None) or ""
887	                ),
888	            )
889	
890	    # --- Chunk ---
891	    def put_chunk(self, c: Chunk) -> None:
892	        row = StoredChunk(
893	            id=c.id,
894	            artifact_id=c.artifact_id,
895	            start_offset=c.start_offset,
896	            end_offset=c.end_offset,
897	            text=c.text,
898	            metadata_json=json.dumps(c.metadata, ensure_ascii=False),
899	        )
900	        with self.session() as s:
901	            existing = s.get(StoredChunk, c.id)
902	            if existing:
903	                for k, v in row.model_dump(exclude={"id"}).items():
904	                    setattr(existing, k, v)
905	                s.add(existing)
906	            else:
907	                s.add(row)
908	            s.commit()
909	
910	    def get_chunk(self, chunk_id: str) -> Optional[Chunk]:
911	        with self.session() as s:
912	            r = s.get(StoredChunk, chunk_id)
913	            if r is None:
914	                return None
915	            meta: dict[str, str] = {}
916	            if r.metadata_json:
917	                try:
918	                    raw = json.loads(r.metadata_json)
919	                    if isinstance(raw, dict):
920	                        meta = {str(k): str(v) for k, v in raw.items()}
921	                except json.JSONDecodeError:
922	                    meta = {}
923	            return Chunk(
924	                id=r.id,
925	                artifact_id=r.artifact_id,
926	                start_offset=r.start_offset,
927	                end_offset=r.end_offset,
928	                text=r.text,
929	                metadata=meta,
930	            )
931	
932	    # --- Claim ---
933	    def put_claim(self, c: Claim) -> None:
934	        row = StoredClaim(id=c.id, payload_json=c.model_dump_json())
935	        with self.session() as s:
936	            existing = s.get(StoredClaim, c.id)
937	            if existing:
938	                existing.payload_json = row.payload_json
939	                s.add(existing)
940	            else:
941	                s.add(row)
942	            s.commit()
943	        try:
944	            from noosphere.claim_extractor import run_scaled_coherence_for_claim
945	
946	            run_scaled_coherence_for_claim(c, self)
947	        except Exception:
948	            pass
949	
950	    def get_claim(self, claim_id: str) -> Optional[Claim]:
951	        with self.session() as s:
952	            r = s.get(StoredClaim, claim_id)
953	            if r is None:
954	                return None
955	            return Claim.model_validate_json(r.payload_json)
956	
957	    # --- Embedding ---
958	    def put_embedding(
959	        self,
960	        *,
961	        embedding_id: str,
962	        model_name: str,
963	        text_sha256: str,
964	        vector: list[float],
965	        ref_claim_id: str = "",
966	    ) -> None:
967	        if np is None:
968	            raise ImportError(
969	                "NumPy is required for embedding persistence"

1	"""Shared pytest fixtures.
2	
3	Two responsibilities:
4	
5	1. Audio fixture synthesis — ``tiny_audio_fixture`` synthesizes a short
6	   English .m4a on demand (via macOS ``say`` + ffmpeg) rather than
7	   shipping bytes in git. Keeps the repo lean and sidesteps the
8	   "who owns this recording?" IP question. Cached under
9	   ``tests/fixtures/`` so repeat runs are free.
10	
11	2. Codex DB factories — ``fake_codex_db`` spins up a throwaway SQLite
12	   instance seeded from ``fixtures/minimal_codex_schema.sql`` so the
13	   ingest pipeline can run without a live Postgres. ``upload_factory``
14	   inserts rows; ``sqlite_url_for`` turns a connection into the URL
15	   that ``ingest_from_codex(codex_db_url=...)`` expects.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import shutil
21	import sqlite3
22	import subprocess
23	from pathlib import Path
24	from uuid import uuid4
25	
26	import pytest
27	
28	
29	_FIXTURES_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures"
30	_TINY_AUDIO = _FIXTURES_DIR / "tiny_audio.m4a"
31	_TINY_PHRASE = "Noosphere audio extractor test, one two three."
32	_SCHEMA_FILE = _FIXTURES_DIR / "minimal_codex_schema.sql"
33	
34	
35	@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
36	def tiny_audio_fixture() -> Path:
37	    """Return the path to a tiny .m4a clip, synthesizing on first use.
38	
39	    Skips the calling test if neither ``say`` nor ``ffmpeg`` are on PATH.
40	    """
41	    if _TINY_AUDIO.exists() and _TINY_AUDIO.stat().st_size > 0:
42	        return _TINY_AUDIO
43	
44	    say = shutil.which("say")
45	    ffmpeg = shutil.which("ffmpeg")
46	    if say is None or ffmpeg is None:
47	        pytest.skip(
48	            "tiny_audio fixture requires macOS `say` + ffmpeg to synthesize; "
49	            "install ffmpeg or ship a pre-recorded clip at "
50	            f"{_TINY_AUDIO} to enable."
51	        )
52	
53	    _FIXTURES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
54	    aiff = _TINY_AUDIO.with_suffix(".aiff")
55	    try:
56	        subprocess.run(
57	            [say, "-o", str(aiff), "--data-format=LEI16@22050", _TINY_PHRASE],
58	            check=True, capture_output=True,
59	        )
60	        subprocess.run(
61	            [
62	                ffmpeg, "-hide_banner", "-loglevel", "error", "-y",
63	                "-i", str(aiff),
64	                "-ac", "1", "-ar", "16000", "-c:a", "aac", "-b:a", "48k",
65	                str(_TINY_AUDIO),
66	            ],
67	            check=True, capture_output=True,
68	        )
69	    finally:
70	        if aiff.exists():
71	            aiff.unlink()
72	
73	    return _TINY_AUDIO
74	
75	
76	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77	# Codex DB factories (SQLite-backed, test-only)
78	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
79	
80	
81	def _sqlite_path_of(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> str:
82	    """Return the on-disk path backing a sqlite connection's ``main`` DB."""
83	    for row in conn.execute("PRAGMA database_list").fetchall():
84	        # row: (seq, name, file) regardless of row_factory.
85	        name = row["name"] if isinstance(row, sqlite3.Row) else row[1]
86	        file_ = row["file"] if isinstance(row, sqlite3.Row) else row[2]
87	        if name == "main":
88	            return file_
89	    raise RuntimeError("could not derive sqlite path from connection")
90	
91	
92	@pytest.fixture
93	def fake_codex_db(tmp_path):
94	    """A throwaway SQLite DB seeded with the ingest-relevant Codex schema."""
95	    path = tmp_path / "codex.db"
96	    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(path))
97	    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
98	    conn.executescript(_SCHEMA_FILE.read_text())
99	    # Seed a minimal Organization row so the optional slug-filter check
100	    # (and any future referential-integrity probes) have something to find.
101	    conn.execute(
102	        'INSERT INTO "Organization" (id, slug, name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)',
103	        ("org_1", "test-org", "Test Org"),
104	    )
105	    conn.commit()
106	    try:
107	        yield conn
108	    finally:
109	        conn.close()
110	
111	
112	@pytest.fixture
113	def codex_sqlite_url(fake_codex_db) -> str:
114	    """The ``sqlite://`` URL that ``ingest_from_codex(codex_db_url=...)``
115	    understands (teaches the bridge to route through sqlite3 instead of
116	    psycopg2). Paired 1:1 with ``fake_codex_db``."""
117	    return f"sqlite://{_sqlite_path_of(fake_codex_db)}"
118	
119	
120	def _insert_upload(
121	    conn: sqlite3.Connection,
122	    *,
123	    mime: str,
124	    text: str | None = None,
125	    source_type: str = "written",
126	    file_path: str | None = None,
127	    file_size: int = 0,
128	    original_name: str = "test",

"""Shared pytest fixtures.

Two responsibilities:

1. Audio fixture synthesis — ``tiny_audio_fixture`` synthesizes a short
   English .m4a on demand (via macOS ``say`` + ffmpeg) rather than
   shipping bytes in git. Keeps the repo lean and sidesteps the
   "who owns this recording?" IP question. Cached under
   ``tests/fixtures/`` so repeat runs are free.

2. Codex DB factories — ``fake_codex_db`` spins up a throwaway SQLite
   instance seeded from ``fixtures/minimal_codex_schema.sql`` so the
   ingest pipeline can run without a live Postgres. ``upload_factory``
   inserts rows; ``sqlite_url_for`` turns a connection into the URL
   that ``ingest_from_codex(codex_db_url=...)`` expects.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import shutil
import sqlite3
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from uuid import uuid4

import pytest


_FIXTURES_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures"
_TINY_AUDIO = _FIXTURES_DIR / "tiny_audio.m4a"
_TINY_PHRASE = "Noosphere audio extractor test, one two three."
_SCHEMA_FILE = _FIXTURES_DIR / "minimal_codex_schema.sql"


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def tiny_audio_fixture() -> Path:
    """Return the path to a tiny .m4a clip, synthesizing on first use.

    Skips the calling test if neither ``say`` nor ``ffmpeg`` are on PATH.
    """
    if _TINY_AUDIO.exists() and _TINY_AUDIO.stat().st_size > 0:
        return _TINY_AUDIO

    say = shutil.which("say")
    ffmpeg = shutil.which("ffmpeg")
    if say is None or ffmpeg is None:
        pytest.skip(
            "tiny_audio fixture requires macOS `say` + ffmpeg to synthesize; "
            "install ffmpeg or ship a pre-recorded clip at "
            f"{_TINY_AUDIO} to enable."
        )

    _FIXTURES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    aiff = _TINY_AUDIO.with_suffix(".aiff")
    try:
        subprocess.run(
            [say, "-o", str(aiff), "--data-format=LEI16@22050", _TINY_PHRASE],
            check=True, capture_output=True,
        )
        subprocess.run(
            [
                ffmpeg, "-hide_banner", "-loglevel", "error", "-y",
                "-i", str(aiff),
                "-ac", "1", "-ar", "16000", "-c:a", "aac", "-b:a", "48k",
                str(_TINY_AUDIO),
            ],
            check=True, capture_output=True,
        )
    finally:
        if aiff.exists():
            aiff.unlink()

    return _TINY_AUDIO


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Codex DB factories (SQLite-backed, test-only)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _sqlite_path_of(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> str:
    """Return the on-disk path backing a sqlite connection's ``main`` DB."""
    for row in conn.execute("PRAGMA database_list").fetchall():
        # row: (seq, name, file) regardless of row_factory.
        name = row["name"] if isinstance(row, sqlite3.Row) else row[1]
        file_ = row["file"] if isinstance(row, sqlite3.Row) else row[2]
        if name == "main":
            return file_
    raise RuntimeError("could not derive sqlite path from connection")


@pytest.fixture
def fake_codex_db(tmp_path):
    """A throwaway SQLite DB seeded with the ingest-relevant Codex schema."""
    path = tmp_path / "codex.db"
    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(path))
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    conn.executescript(_SCHEMA_FILE.read_text())
    # Seed a minimal Organization row so the optional slug-filter check
    # (and any future referential-integrity probes) have something to find.
    conn.execute(
        'INSERT INTO "Organization" (id, slug, name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)',
        ("org_1", "test-org", "Test Org"),
    )
    conn.commit()
    try:
        yield conn
    finally:
        conn.close()


@pytest.fixture
def codex_sqlite_url(fake_codex_db) -> str:
    """The ``sqlite://`` URL that ``ingest_from_codex(codex_db_url=...)``
    understands (teaches the bridge to route through sqlite3 instead of
    psycopg2). Paired 1:1 with ``fake_codex_db``."""
    return f"sqlite://{_sqlite_path_of(fake_codex_db)}"


def _insert_upload(
    conn: sqlite3.Connection,
    *,
    mime: str,
    text: str | None = None,
    source_type: str = "written",
    file_path: str | None = None,
    file_size: int = 0,
    original_name: str = "test",
    title: str = "test",
    org_id: str = "org_1",
    founder_id: str = "u_1",
) -> str:
    uid = f"cx_{uuid4().hex[:22]}"
    conn.execute(
        'INSERT INTO "Upload" '
        '(id, "organizationId", "founderId", title, "sourceType", "textContent", status, '
        ' "mimeType", "originalName", "filePath", "fileSize") '
        'VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)',
        (
            uid,
            org_id,
            founder_id,
            title,
            source_type,
            text,
            "pending",
            mime,
            original_name,
            file_path,
            file_size,
        ),
    )
    conn.commit()
    return uid


@pytest.fixture
def upload_factory(fake_codex_db):
    def _make(**kw) -> str:
        return _insert_upload(fake_codex_db, **kw)
    return _make


@pytest.fixture
def scratch_binary_fixture(tmp_path) -> Path:
    """A small on-disk file used when the ingest pipeline needs *some* bytes
    to fetch but the contents are irrelevant (e.g. extractor is stubbed,
    or the MIME is unsupported and dispatch fails before parsing)."""
    p = tmp_path / "scratch.bin"
    p.write_bytes(b"\x00\x01\x02 scratch payload")
    return p


@pytest.fixture
def forecasts_seed():

    from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
    from decimal import Decimal

    st = Store.from_database_url("sqlite:///:memory:")
    now = datetime(2026, 4, 29, 12, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    org_id = "org_forecasts"

    poly_market = ForecastMarket(
        id="forecast_market_poly",

    st.put_forecast_market(poly_market)

    poly_prediction = ForecastPrediction(
        id="forecast_prediction_poly",

        probability_yes=Decimal("0.680000"),

        confidence_high=Decimal("0.760000"),

    kalshi_prediction = ForecastPrediction(

        headline="The macro setup leans slightly below consensus",
        reasoning="Fixture reasoning grounded in a source-citation path.",
        status=ForecastPredictionStatus.PUBLISHED,

        model_name="fixture-model",
        created_at=now + timedelta(minutes=1),
    )
    st.put_forecast_prediction(poly_prediction)
    st.put_forecast_prediction(kalshi_prediction)

    poly_bet = ForecastBet(
        id="forecast_bet_poly_yes",
        prediction_id=poly_prediction.id,
        organization_id=org_id,

        exchange=ForecastExchange.POLYMARKET,
        side=ForecastBetSide.YES,
        stake_usd=Decimal("100.00"),
        entry_price=Decimal("0.610000"),
        status=ForecastBetStatus.FILLED,
        created_at=now + timedelta(minutes=2),
    )
    kalshi_bet = ForecastBet(
        id="forecast_bet_kalshi_no",
        prediction_id=kalshi_prediction.id,
        organization_id=org_id,
        mode=ForecastBetMode.PAPER,
        exchange=ForecastExchange.KALSHI,
        side=ForecastBetSide.NO,
        stake_usd=Decimal("100.00"),
        entry_price=Decimal("0.530000"),
        status=ForecastBetStatus.FILLED,
        created_at=now + timedelta(minutes=3),
    )
    st.put_forecast_bet(poly_bet)
    st.put_forecast_bet(kalshi_bet)

    return {
        "store": st,

        "now": now,

    }

name: str
    version: str

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="forbid", frozen=True)


class MethodInvocation(BaseModel):
    """Record of a method invocation for audit trail."""

    id: str
    method_id: str
    input_hash: str
    output_hash: str
    started_at: datetime
    ended_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    succeeded: bool
    error_kind: Optional[str] = None
    correlation_id: str
    tenant_id: str

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="forbid", frozen=True)


# ── Round 3: Ledger ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


class Actor(BaseModel):
    """An actor in the system: human, method, or agent."""

    kind: Literal["human", "method", "agent"]
    id: str
    display_name: str

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="forbid", frozen=True)


class ContextMeta(BaseModel):
    """Context metadata for ledger entries."""

    tenant_id: str
    correlation_id: str
    orchestrator_run_id: Optional[str] = None

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra="forbid", frozen=True)


class LedgerEntry(BaseModel):
    """Immutable audit ledger entry."""

    entry_id: str
    prev_hash: str
    timestamp: datetime
    actor: Actor
    method_id: Optional[str] = None
    inputs_hash: str
    outputs_hash: str
    inputs_ref: str
    outputs_ref: str
    context: ContextMeta
    signature: str

<ActionsBar conclusionId={id} />

        <TabNav
          basePath={`/conclusions/${id}`}
          current={activeTab}
          tabs={TABS as unknown as ReadonlyArray<{ id: string; label: string }>}
        />

        <section style={{ marginTop: "1.5rem" }}>
          {activeTab === "overview" ? (
            <OverviewTab
              text={conclusion.text}
              supportIds={supportIds}
              evidenceIds={evidenceIds}
              dissentIds={dissentIds}
              idTextMap={idTextMap}
              sources={sources}
            />
          ) : activeTab === "provenance" ? (
            <ProvenanceTab conclusionId={id} />
          ) : activeTab === "cascade" ? (
            <CascadeTab conclusionId={id} />
          ) : activeTab === "peer" ? (
            <PeerReviewTab conclusionId={id} />
          ) : activeTab === "related" ? (
            <RelatedTab conclusionId={id} />
          ) : (
            <HistoryTab conclusionId={id} />
          )}
        </section>

        <nav
          style={{
            marginTop: "2rem",
            display: "flex",
            alignItems: "center",
            gap: "1rem",
            flexWrap: "wrap",
          }}
        >
          <Link
            href="/knowledge?tab=conclusions"
            style={{
              fontFamily: "'Cinzel', serif",
              fontSize: "0.7rem",
              letterSpacing: "0.1em",
              color: "var(--parchment-dim)",
              textDecoration: "none",
            }}

/**
 * Founder role ladder + permission predicates.
 *
 * Every founder in the database carries a `role: string` column with one
 * of three values. The string is intentionally free-form (no enum at the
 * Prisma level) so adding a new tier later doesn't require a migration —
 * the SQL column stays `TEXT`, the validation lives here at the
 * application boundary.
 *
 * The ladder
 * ----------
 *
 *   admin    → full read + write + can change other founders' roles
 *              (the "alpha" tier; the only role that can promote /
 *              demote others). At least one admin must always exist
 *              per organization — see `/api/founders/[id]/role` for
 *              the self-demotion guard.
 *
 *   founder  → full read + write. Can upload files, publish posts,
 *              run peer reviews, request deletions, etc. The default
 *              for everyone added via `scripts/add-founder.ts`. This
 *              is the standard-issue role.
 *
 *   viewer   → read-only. Can navigate the entire portal — library,
 *              dashboard, conclusions, /ask, /post/<slug>, etc. — and
 *              read every artifact the org has produced, but every
 *              write affordance (upload, publish, delete, peer-review
 *              vote, request-deletion, dashboard dismissal, …) is
 *              disabled in the UI and rejected with 403 at the API.
 *              Useful for advisors, auditors, board observers, future
 *              hires you want to read-only-onboard before flipping
 *              them to founder.
 *
 * Where this is used
 * ------------------
 *
 *   • API gates: every write endpoint calls `canWrite(founder.role)`
 *     and 403s if false. The check happens AFTER auth (so an
 *     unauthenticated caller still gets 401, not 403) and BEFORE any
 *     side-effecting work.
 *
 *   • UI gates: server components pass the role into client components
 *     via the (authed) layout's `getFounder().role`. Client components
 *     hide write affordances based on the predicates here. The UI gate
 *     is a UX nicety; the API gate is the actual security boundary.
 *
 *   • Founder management: only admins see the /founders/manage page
 *     and `canManageFounders(role)` returns true.
 *
 * Why three roles instead of N permission flags
 * ---------------------------------------------
 *
 * The Codex's threat model is "trusted firm members with different
 * levels of write authority", not "complex multi-tenant marketplace".
 * Three labelled tiers are easier to reason about than a permissions
 * matrix and cover every case the product has surfaced so far.
 * Per-action permission flags can be layered on later if a real
 * use-case demands them.
 */

export type FounderRole = "admin" | "founder" | "viewer";

const VALID_ROLES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
  "admin",
  "founder",
  "viewer",
]);

/**
 * Type-narrow + validate a string from the wire / DB into our role
 * union. Defaults are intentionally NOT applied here — callers should
 * decide whether an unknown role means "block" (most write APIs) or
 * "treat as viewer" (most UI surfaces).
 */
export function isValidRole(value: unknown): value is FounderRole {
  return typeof value === "string" && VALID_ROLES.has(value);
}

/**
 * Normalise a role string to a known value. Anything we don't
 * recognise — empty string, legacy uppercase, accidental whitespace,
 * unmigrated old enum — collapses to the safest fallback ("viewer"),
 * so a corrupted DB row can't accidentally elevate someone.
 */
export function normaliseRole(value: unknown): FounderRole {
  if (!isValidRole(value)) return "viewer";
  return value;
}

/** Every signed-in role can read. */
export function canRead(role: string): boolean {
  return isValidRole(role);
}

/**
 * "Write" = anything that changes durable state on behalf of this
 * founder. Uploading, publishing, deleting, requesting deletion,
 * casting a peer-review verdict, dismissing a dashboard item, etc.
 * Read-only API endpoints (/api/library, /api/upload/:id GET,
 * /api/ask) are NOT gated by this — viewers can see everything
 * they're authorised to see in their org.
 */
export function canWrite(role: string): boolean {
  return role === "admin" || role === "founder";
}

/**
 * Only admins can change other founders' roles. The /founders/manage
 * page checks this server-side; the PATCH /api/founders/:id/role
 * endpoint re-checks it and additionally guards against demoting the
 * last admin in the organisation (so an org can't accidentally lock
 * itself out of role management).
 */
export function canManageFounders(role: string): boolean {
  return role === "admin";
}

/** Admin-only contact inbox access. Kept separate so policy can diverge later. */
export function canReadContactInbox(role: string): boolean {
  return canManageFounders(role);
}

/**
 * Human-readable description for the role ladder. Surfaced in the
 * /founders/manage UI dropdown so the admin doesn't have to remember
 * what each label implies.
 */
export const ROLE_DESCRIPTIONS: Record<FounderRole, string> = {
  admin:
    "Full access. Can upload, publish, delete, AND change other founders' roles.",
  founder:
    "Standard access. Can upload, publish, delete, run peer reviews. Cannot change others' roles.",
  viewer:
    "Read-only. Can navigate the entire portal but every write action is blocked.",
};

/**
 * Standard JSON shape for the 403 a write endpoint returns when the
 * caller's role is below `canWrite`. Surfaces a stable error string
 * the client can match on if it wants to render a special "ask an
 * admin to upgrade you" affordance.
 */
export const WRITE_FORBIDDEN_RESPONSE = {
  error:
    "This account is read-only. An admin in your organisation must upgrade you to 'founder' or 'admin' before you can take this action.",
  code: "viewer_write_forbidden" as const,
};

/**
 * Standard JSON shape for the 403 the founder-management endpoint
 * returns when a non-admin tries to change someone's role.
 */
export const MANAGE_FORBIDDEN_RESPONSE = {
  error:
    "Only admins can change founder roles. Ask an admin in your organisation to make the change for you.",
  code: "manage_founders_forbidden" as const,
};

content.content:
441	model Conclusion {
442	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
443	  organizationId String
444	
445	  /// Stable id if synced from / produced for Noosphere
446	  noosphereId String? @unique
447	  text        String
448	
449	  // ── Dedup key ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
450	  // `normalizedText` is `LOWER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(text, '\s+', ' ')))`
451	  // computed once at ingest time. The ingest pipeline checks for an
452	  // existing Conclusion in the same org with a matching normalizedText;
453	  // if one exists, it adds the current upload as an ADDITIONAL source
454	  // via ConclusionSource rather than inserting a duplicate row. That's
455	  // what makes deletion correct: when two uploads produced the same
456	  // claim and we delete one, the conclusion stays — it's still
457	  // supported by the other source.
458	  //
459	  // Nullable so the column can be added to an existing DB without a
460	  // rewrite; the migration back-fills every existing row. Indexed
461	  // `(organizationId, normalizedText)` because every lookup is scoped
462	  // to a single org.
463	  normalizedText         String?
464	  confidenceTier         String // firm | founder | open (ConfidenceTier)
465	  rationale              String   @default("")
466	  supportingPrincipleIds String   @default("[]") // JSON string[]
467	  evidenceChainClaimIds  String   @default("[]")
468	  dissentClaimIds        String   @default("[]")
469	  confidence             Float    @default(0)
470	  topicHint              String   @default("") // UI filter / display
471	  createdAt              DateTime @default(now())
472	
473	  /// JSON-serialized float[] embedding produced by Noosphere's sentence
474	  /// encoder at ingest time. Null for conclusions ingested before this
475	  /// column was added; the semantic explorer backfills on demand.
476	  embeddingJson String?
477	
478	  attributedFounderId String?
479	  attributedFounder   Founder?                    @relation("ConclusionFounder", fields: [attributedFounderId], references: [id])
480	  organization        Organization                @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
481	  publicationReviews  PublicationReview[]
482	  sources             ConclusionSource[]
483	  methodologyProfiles MethodologyProfile[]
484	  /// Optional MQS row (1:1). Built by `noosphere mqs backfill`; re-runnable
485	  /// without rewriting the underlying methodology profile.
486	  methodologyQualityScore MethodologyQualityScore?
487	  conclusionMethods   ConclusionMethod[]
488	  domainBoundVerdicts DomainBoundVerdict[]
489	  recalibrationOverride RecalibrationOverride?
490	  deletionRequests    ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
491	  dashboardDismissals DashboardDismissal[]
492	
493	  /// Conclusion edit timestamp; the public MQS pill is only shown when the
494	  /// MQS row's `scoredAt` is at least this recent.
495	  updatedAt           DateTime?                   @updatedAt
496	
497	  @@index([organizationId])
498	  @@index([organizationId, normalizedText])
499	}
500	
501	/// Many-to-many bridge Conclusion ↔ Upload.
502	///
503	/// Every conclusion that came out of the ingest pipeline has at least
504	/// one row here tying it back to the upload it was extracted from.
505	/// When the SAME normalized text appears in multiple uploads, the
506	/// ingest pipeline reuses the existing Conclusion row and adds a new
507	/// ConclusionSource row per additional upload, so the upstream count
508	/// can drop from 2 → 1 → 0 as uploads are deleted.
509	///
510	/// Delete semantics (enforced by the app-side delete handlers, NOT by
511	/// FK cascade — we soft-delete uploads, so the FK's ON DELETE CASCADE
512	/// would not fire anyway):
513	///
514	///   * When an upload is soft-deleted, every ConclusionSource row
515	///     with that uploadId is hard-deleted.
516	///   * After that, any Conclusion with zero remaining rows is
517	///     hard-deleted too (the claim's last source is gone).
518	///   * Conclusions with sources from other uploads remain, just
519	///     with one fewer row.
520	///
521	/// The FK cascade below still serves as a safety net for the rare
522	/// case of a hard-delete (org takedown, privacy-law purge).
523	model ConclusionSource {
524	  conclusionId String
525	  uploadId     String
526	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
527	
528	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
529	  upload     Upload     @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
530	
531	  @@id([conclusionId, uploadId])
532	  @@index([conclusionId])
533	  @@index([uploadId])
534	}
535	
536	// ── Publication (SP10) — review queue + versioned public snapshots ───────────
537	
538	/// Internal gate before a conclusion may appear on the public site.
539	model PublicationReview {
540	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
541	  organizationId String
542	  conclusionId   String
543	
544	  /// queued | in_review | needs_revision | declined | published
545	  status        String @default("queued")
546	  /// JSON: { metaAnalysisOk, adversarialEngagedOk, clarityOk, noLeakageOk }
547	  checklistJson String @default("{}")
548	  reviewerNotes String @default("")
549	  declineReason String @default("")
550	  revisionAsk   String @default("")
551	
552	  reviewerFounderId String?
553	  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
554	  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
555	
556	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
557	  target       Conclusion   @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
558	  reviewer     Founder?     @relation("PublicationReviewer", fields: [reviewerFounderId], references: [id])
559	
560	  @@index([organizationId])
561	  @@index([conclusionId])
562	  @@index([status])
563	}
564	
565	/// Immutable-ish public snapshot (new row per material revision).
566	model PublishedConclusion {
567	  id                 String @id @default(cuid())
568	  organizationId     String
569	  sourceConclusionId String
570	
571	  slug    String
572	  version Int    @default(1)
573	  /// CONCLUSION for reviewed firm conclusions; ARTICLE for generated longform essays.
574	  kind    String @default("CONCLUSION")
575	
576	  /// Headline discounted confidence (SP05); stated founder/model confidence alongside in JSON.
577	  discountedConfidence      Float
578	  statedConfidence          Float  @default(0)
579	  calibrationDiscountReason String @default("")
580	
581	  /// Full page payload: evidence, exit conditions, adversarial, voice map, timeline, citations.
582	  payloadJson String @default("{}")
583	
584	  /// Zenodo DOI for this revision (minted on first publish of revision).
585	  doi            String @default("")
586	  zenodoRecordId String @default("")
587	
588	  publishedAt DateTime @default(now())
589	
590	  organization Organization     @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
591	  responses    PublicResponse[]
592	
593	  @@unique([slug, version])
594	  @@index([organizationId])
595	  @@index([slug])
596	  @@index([kind, publishedAt])
597	}
598	
599	// ── Methodology profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
600	
601	/// A durable description of how a piece of material reasons, not merely what it
602	/// concludes. Noosphere writes these from transcripts/uploads and reanalysis
603	/// scripts; publication review can attach them to public conclusions.
604	model MethodologyProfile {
605	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
606	  organizationId String
607	  uploadId       String?
608	  conclusionId   String?
609	
610	  /// UPLOAD when derived from a transcript/artifact; CONCLUSION when derived

1281	model DriftEvent {
1282	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
1283	  organizationId String
1284	
1285	  noosphereId                 String?  @unique
